title: Research Assistant Position Inquiry
transcript: Student: Professor Gupta, I saw the email you sent about hiring a research assistant for your linguistics study. I'm very interested in applying. Professor: Great, I'm glad you reached out. What aspect of the project interests you? Student: I'm fascinated by how bilingual children acquire grammar. I took your psycholinguistics course last semester and really enjoyed it. Professor: I remember you. You wrote an excellent paper on language acquisition. Do you have any previous research experience? Student: I helped a graduate student with data collection for a study on second language pronunciation last year. I did transcription and basic data entry. Professor: That's good experience. The main tasks for this position would be recruiting participants, conducting interviews in both English and Spanish, and transcribing audio recordings. Are you fluent in Spanish? Student: Yes, I grew up speaking Spanish at home. I'm comfortable with both academic and conversational Spanish. Professor: Excellent, that's a key requirement. The position is about ten hours per week with flexible scheduling. Would that work with your class schedule? Student: I think so. I only have classes in the mornings this semester, so afternoons would be open. Professor: Perfect. Could you send me your resume and the names of two references? I'd like to review them and set up a brief follow-up meeting next week. Student: Absolutely. I'll send everything by tomorrow morning. Thank you for considering me.

questions.stem: Why does the student visit Professor Gupta?
options: To ask about a grade from last semester
To express interest in a research assistant position
To request a topic change for a paper
To get advice on graduate school applications
questions.explanation: The student saw the professor's email about a research assistant position and visits to express interest and discuss the role.

questions.stem: What is a key requirement for the research assistant position?
options: Fluency in both English and Spanish
Availability to work full time during the summer
questions.explanation: The professor mentions that conducting interviews in both English and Spanish is a main task, and confirms the student is fluent in Spanish as a key requirement.

Great, I'm glad you reached out. What aspect of the project interests you?
I'm fascinated by how bilingual children acquire grammar. I took your psycholinguistics course last semester and really enjoyed it.
I remember you. You wrote an excellent paper on language acquisition. Do you have any previous research experience?
I helped a graduate student with data collection for a study on second language pronunciation last year. I did transcription and basic data entry.

That's good experience. The main tasks for this position would be recruiting participants, conducting interviews in both English and Spanish, and transcribing audio recordings. Are you fluent in Spanish?
Yes, I grew up speaking Spanish at home. I'm comfortable with both academic and conversational Spanish.
Excellent, that's a key requirement. The position is about ten hours per week with flexible scheduling. Would that work with your class schedule?
I think so. I only have classes in the mornings this semester, so afternoons would be open.
Perfect. Could you send me your resume and the names of two references? I'd like to review them and set up a brief follow-up meeting next week.
Absolutely. I'll send everything by tomorrow morning. Thank you for considering me.
